seoTitle: Stony Creek vs Storm King — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Stony Creek and Storm King. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Stony Creek vs Storm King: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Stony Creek and Storm King.
heading: Stony Creek vs Storm King — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Stony Creek, Queensland and Storm King, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Stony Creek has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Storm King (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Stony Creek or Storm King safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Stony Creek has a flood risk score of 53/100, while Storm King scores 58/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Stony Creek scores 49/100 for bushfire risk. Storm King scores 57/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Stony Creek or Storm King?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Stony Creek has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.08% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
